# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3  # the API version of your resources, set it manually by hand or automatically from kubectl command line tool options if not defined explicitly  
kind: VirtualService   
metadata:    
 name: sample-mesh                      # Name for this resource as you wish in Kubernetes (optional)     
 namespace: default                     # Namespace where your resources should reside, defaults to the current context's namespace unless specified otherwise. 
spec:             
 http:             # The configuration section defining rules and requests that make up a rule set   
 - route:            # Rule based routing  
     destination:      # Destination definition in terms of service name or ID (optional)         
       host: services-a        # Host attribute for the target client. Mandatory if not specified as is default             
 routes:           # Array of weighted destinations to which traffic should be routed when using this rule 
 - destination:      # Destination definition in terms of service name or ID (optional)         
   host: services-b       # Host attribute for the target client. Mandatory if not specified as is default    `
